lede: A look at April 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Violet McGraw and David J. C. MacKay, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2025 Pahalgam attack
events.desc: At least 26 people are killed in a terrorist attack on a group of tourists in Pahalgam, Jammu and Kashmir. The Resistance Front (TRF), an offshoot of the Pakistan-based militant group Lashkar-e-Taiba claimed responsibility for the attack.

events.title: 2020 Eastern Freeway truck crash
events.desc: Four police officers are killed after being struck by a truck on the Eastern Freeway in Melbourne while speaking to a speeding driver, marking the largest loss of police lives in Victoria Police history.

events.title: Paris Agreement
events.desc: The Paris Agreement is signed, an agreement to help fight global warming.

events.title: San Diego
events.desc: Chicano residents in San Diego, California occupy a site under the Coronado Bridge, leading to the creation of Chicano Park.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: The Chinese People's Volunteer Army begin assaulting positions defended by the Royal Australian Regiment and the Princess Patricia's Canadian Light Infantry at the Battle of Kapyong.

events.title: Jasenovac concentration camp
events.desc: World War II: Prisoners at the Jasenovac concentration camp revolt. Five hundred twenty are killed and around eighty escape.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The 1st Air Commando Group using Sikorsky R-4 helicopters stage the first use of helicopters in combat with combat search and rescue operations in the China Burma India Theater.

events.title: Land Rush of 1889
events.desc: At noon, thousands rush to claim land in the Land Rush of 1889. Within hours the cities of Oklahoma City and Guthrie are formed with populations of at least 10,000.

events.title: Pedro Álvares Cabral
events.desc: Portuguese navigator Pedro Álvares Cabral lands in Brazil (discovery of Brazil).

events.title: Pope Gregory VII
events.desc: Election of Pope Gregory VII following the death of Pope Alexander II.

births.name: John Buchanan (Canadian politician)
births.line: Canadian lawyer and politician, 20th Premier of Nova Scotia (died 2019)

births.name: William IV, Grand Duke of Luxembourg

deaths.text: Len Goodman, English ballroom dancer and television personality
Jack Cardiff, British cinematographer, director and photographer
Richard Nixon, 37th President of the United States

observances.text: Christian feast day: Acepsimas of Hnaita and companions (Catholic Church)
